description: §6.2 decl-RHS canonical form negative — the SAME three decls as decl-rhs-interp-wrapped-pos with the `${ }` wrapper REMOVED, i.e. the canonical bare-expression RHS. E-DECL-RHS-INTERP-WRAPPED stays silent and the cells compute normally. This is the wrapped-vs-bare contrast; the runtime half proves the bare form is the one that actually evaluates.
rationale: §6.2 (SPEC.md:2217): "All three RHS shapes take a bare expression as the right-hand side." The bare-expression RHS IS the one canonical form for plain (Shape 1), derived (Shape 3), and typed decls, so no diagnostic fires. Per §6.2 Shape 3 the derived cells recompute from their dependencies: @count=2 → doubled=4, tripled=6; @names has 2 elements → total=2.
